label: BigQuery
description: Query and analyze data in Google BigQuery using a service account or OAuth credentials.

form.title: Connect BigQuery
form.subtitle: Grant Anton access to run queries and read data from your BigQuery project.

methods.label: Service account JSON key
methods.description: Paste a Google Cloud service account JSON key. The simplest, most portable path — no browser flow required, works in any environment.
methods.how_to:
## Service account JSON key

The fastest way to connect. You create a service account in Google Cloud, download its JSON key, and paste it below.

### Steps

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and select the project that owns your BigQuery datasets.
2. Go to **IAM & Admin → Service Accounts → Create service account**. Give it a descriptive name like `anton-bigquery`.
3. On the **Grant this service account access to project** step, assign one of these roles:
   - **BigQuery Data Viewer** + **BigQuery Job User** — read-only queries (recommended minimum).
   - **BigQuery User** — allows creating jobs and reading data.
   - **BigQuery Admin** — full control (use only if Anton needs to create/delete tables).
4. Click **Done**. Open the new service account, go to the **Keys** tab.
5. Click **Add key → Create new key → JSON**. A `.json` file downloads automatically.
6. Paste the entire contents of that file into **Service account JSON** below.
7. Enter the **Project ID** of the GCP project (visible in the Cloud Console header, e.g. `my-project-123`).

### Notes

- The JSON key contains a private key — treat it like a password. Rotate it in Cloud Console if you suspect exposure.
- If your datasets live in multiple projects, use the project that billing is attached to as the **Project ID**, and grant the service account cross-project dataset access via IAM in each data project.
- BigQuery queries incur costs on your GCP account — scope the role to the minimum needed.

fields.name: service_account_json
fields.label: Service account JSON
fields.description: Paste the full contents of the JSON key file downloaded from Google Cloud Console → IAM & Admin → Service Accounts → Keys.

fields.name: project_id
fields.label: Project ID
fields.description: The GCP project ID that BigQuery jobs will be billed to. Visible in the Cloud Console header or on the project's dashboard.

fields.name: location
fields.label: Default dataset location
fields.description: The default geographic location for BigQuery jobs. Must match the location of the datasets you query.

methods.label: Sign in with Google (OAuth 2.0)
methods.description: Authorize via a browser popup using your own Google Cloud OAuth client. Best when you want access tied to a specific user identity rather than a shared service account.
methods.how_to:
> **Pick the OAuth client type that matches your Anton shell:**
>
> - **Desktop app (Anton.app on macOS / Windows)** → in *Application type*, pick **Desktop app**. Google auto-allows loopback redirects, so no redirect-URI registration is needed.
> - **Web (browser-hosted Anton)** → pick **Web application** instead, then under *Authorized redirect URIs* add `http://127.0.0.1:26866/v1/connectors/oauth/callback` (substitute your server origin if you're behind a reverse proxy or running on a non-default port).
>
> The walkthrough below was written for the desktop path. If you're on web, read every "Desktop app" reference as "Web application" and make sure the redirect URI above is registered before clicking **Submit**.

## OAuth 2.0 (bring your own OAuth client)

Anton doesn't ship a hosted Google OAuth client yet, so you'll create one in Google Cloud Console and paste the credentials below.

### Steps

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and select your project.
2. Go to **APIs & Services → Library** and enable **BigQuery API** and **Cloud Resource Manager API**.
3. Go to **APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen**:
   - Choose **Internal** (Workspace) or **External**.
   - Add the scopes: `bigquery`, `cloud-platform`, `userinfo.email`.
   - If external, add your Google account as a test user.
4. Go to **APIs & Services → Credentials → Create credentials → OAuth client ID**.
   - Application type: **Desktop app**.
   - Name it `Anton`.
5. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client secret** shown and paste them below.
6. Click **Submit** — Anton opens a browser window for you to sign in and consent.

### Notes

- The OAuth token is tied to your personal Google identity; it respects your IAM permissions on the project.
- If the consent screen shows a warning about unverified apps, click **Advanced → Go to Anton (unsafe)** — this is expected for internal Desktop app clients.
- Refresh tokens are stored locally and can be revoked at [myaccount.google.com/permissions](https://myaccount.google.com/permissions).

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth client ID
fields.description: From Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials. Use a Desktop app OAuth client.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth client secret
fields.description: Paired with the client ID above; copied from the same credential in Cloud Console.

fields.name: project_id
fields.label: Project ID
fields.description: The GCP project ID that BigQuery jobs will be billed to.
